home.heroTitle: Explore the Ultimate Mysteries of Logic

puzzle1.title: Cheryl's Birthday
puzzle1.desc: Can you deduce the actual birthday from just a few conversations? A true game of logic.

puzzle2.title: Birthday Paradox
puzzle2.desc: Intuition says 23 people having the same birthday is rare? Let's reveal the shocking math.

montyHall.title: Monty Hall Problem
montyHall.description: A probability puzzle based on a TV game show. Test if 'switching doors' really gives you a higher win rate!
montyHall.explanationText: Suppose you're on a game show, and you're given the choice of three doors: Behind one door is a car; behind the others, goats. You pick a door, say No. 1, and the host, who knows what's behind the doors, opens another door, say No. 3, which has a goat. He then says to you, 'Do you want to pick door No. 2?' Is it to your advantage to switch your choice? (Answer: Yes! The probability increases from 1/3 to 2/3)
montyHall.idleDesc: Click 'Run Simulation' to see the results
montyHall.executingPy: Loading Python environment (Pyodide)... This may take a few seconds on the first run.

cherylsBirthday.title: Cheryl's Birthday
cherylsBirthday.description: Albert and Bernard just met Cheryl. They want to know her birthday. Follow the conversation step by step to deduce the exact date!
cherylsBirthday.step1Quote: "I don't know when Cheryl's birthday is, but I know that Bernard does not know too."
cherylsBirthday.step1Explain: Since Bernard doesn't know, the day cannot be unique (not 18 or 19). Albert is certain Bernard doesn't know, meaning his month doesn't contain any unique days. So we can eliminate May and June!
cherylsBirthday.step2Quote: "At first I didn't know when Cheryl's birthday is, but I know now."
cherylsBirthday.step2Explain: Bernard knows now from the remaining July and August dates. This means his day must be unique among the remaining dates. So we eliminate the duplicate day (14).
cherylsBirthday.step3Quote: "Then I also know when Cheryl's birthday is."
cherylsBirthday.step3Explain: Albert knows too. If his month was August, he would still have two choices (15 and 17). Therefore, his month must be July. Case closed!

interactive.instruction: Your turn! Click on the date cards to cross out the impossible options.
interactive.success: 🎉 Congratulations! You nailed it! That's Cheryl's birthday!
interactive.error: 🤔 Something's not quite right. Check the dialogue logic again?
